From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian character actor and comedian

| Bombolo | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |

| Died | 21 August 1987(1987-08-21) (aged 56)  Rome, Italy |

| Years active | 1966–1987 |
| Height | 1.68 m (5 ft 6 in) |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/27/G_Bracardi_Bombolo_Nicolini_ANTIFUD17.jpg/250px-G_Bracardi_Bombolo_Nicolini_ANTIFUD17.jpg)](/wiki/File:G_Bracardi_Bombolo_Nicolini_ANTIFUD17.jpg)

[Giorgio Bracardi](/wiki/Giorgio_Bracardi "Giorgio Bracardi"), [Renato Nicolini](/w/index.php?title=Renato_Nicolini&action=edit&redlink=1 "Renato Nicolini (page does not exist)") [[it](https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Renato_Nicolini "it:Renato Nicolini")], and Bombolo, at an anti-fast food demonstration in [Piazza di Spagna](/wiki/Piazza_di_Spagna "Piazza di Spagna"), 20 April 1986

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f1/Bombolo_1983.jpg/250px-Bombolo_1983.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bombolo_1983.jpg)

Bombolo, 1983

**Franco Lechner**, best known as **Bombolo**[[a]](#cite_note-1) (22 May 1931 – 21 August 1987), was an Italian character actor and comedian.

## Life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bombolo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and career")]

Born in [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome") into a poor family, a former peddler of dishes, Lechner was discovered in a tavern by Franco Castellacci and [Pierfrancesco Pingitore](/wiki/Pierfrancesco_Pingitore "Pierfrancesco Pingitore"), who entered him in their cabaret company "Il Bagaglino".[[1]](#cite_note-jsdh-2) He got a large popularity in cinema as the [sidekick](/wiki/Sidekick "Sidekick") of [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian") in a successful series of [poliziottesco](/wiki/Poliziotteschi "Poliziotteschi") films directed by [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci").[[1]](#cite_note-jsdh-2) He was also partner of [Enzo Cannavale](/wiki/Enzo_Cannavale "Enzo Cannavale") in a number of comedy films.[[2]](#cite_note-fc-3) He died at age 56 as a result of complications following a major surgical operation.[[1]](#cite_note-jsdh-2) Even after several decades, younger generations continue to embrace his characteristic slang, particularly his distinctive and iconic "tze tze".[[2]](#cite_note-fc-3)

## Partial filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bombolo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Partial filmography")]

* *Remo e Romolo (Storia di due figli di una lupa)* (1976) – Pappo
* *La prima notte di nozze* (1976)
* *[Hit Squad](/wiki/Hit_Squad_(film) "Hit Squad (film)")* (1976) – Er Trippa
* *[Nerone](/wiki/Nerone_(1977_film) "Nerone (1977 film)")* (1977) – Roscio
* *[Il marito in collegio](/wiki/Il_marito_in_collegio "Il marito in collegio")* (1977) – Killer
* *[Messalina, Messalina](/wiki/Messalina,_Messalina "Messalina, Messalina")* (1977) – Zenturio Bisone
* *[Swindle](/wiki/Swindle_(1977_film) "Swindle (1977 film)")* (1977) – Franco 'Venticello' Bertarelli
* *[Little Italy](/wiki/Little_Italy_(1978_film) "Little Italy (1978 film)")* (1978) – Venticello
* *Scherzi da prete* (1978) – Bombolo
* *[Assassinio sul Tevere](/wiki/Assassinio_sul_Tevere "Assassinio sul Tevere")* (1979) – Franco 'Venticello' Bertarelli
* *Tutti a squola* (1979) – Bombolo il bidello
* *[L'imbranato](/wiki/L%27imbranato "L'imbranato")* (1979) – Bombolo, il genero
* *[Ciao marziano](/wiki/Ciao_marziano "Ciao marziano")* (1980) – Pietro
* *[Girls Will Be Girls](/wiki/Girls_Will_Be_Girls_(1980_film) "Girls Will Be Girls (1980 film)")* (1980) – Giulio Cesare
* *[Delitto a Porta Romana](/wiki/Delitto_a_Porta_Romana "Delitto a Porta Romana")* (1980) – Franco 'Venticello' Bertarelli
* *[Il casinista](/wiki/Il_casinista "Il casinista")* (1980) – Poldo
* *L'amante tutta da scoprire* (1981) – Bombolo
* *[The Week at the Beach](/wiki/The_Week_at_the_Beach "The Week at the Beach")* (1981) – Orazio Canestrari
* *[Uno contro l'altro, praticamente amici](/wiki/Uno_contro_l%27altro,_praticamente_amici "Uno contro l'altro, praticamente amici")* (1981) – Capoccione
* *I carabbinieri* (1981) – Mozzarella
* *[Una vacanza del cactus](/wiki/Una_vacanza_del_cactus "Una vacanza del cactus")* (1981) – Augusto Squarciarelli
* *[Crime at the Chinese Restaurant](/wiki/Crime_at_the_Chinese_Restaurant "Crime at the Chinese Restaurant")* (1981) – Bombolo
* *Miracoloni* (1981) – Taxista
* *Il marito in vacanza* (1981) – Agenore
* *[W la foca](/wiki/W_la_foca "W la foca")* (1982) – Doctor Filippo Patacchiola
* *Attenti a quei P2* (1982) – Bombolo il portiere
* *Il sommergibile più pazzo del mondo* (1982) – Il Postino
* *[Delitto sull'autostrada](/wiki/Delitto_sull%27autostrada "Delitto sull'autostrada")* (1982) – Venticello
* *Sturmtruppen 2 (tutti al fronte)* (1982)
* *La sai l'ultima sui matti?* (1982) – Caciotta
* *È forte un casino!* (1982) – Cicciobello
* *Un jeans e una maglietta* (1983)
* *Sfrattato cerca casa equo canone* (1983) – Maciste
* *La discoteca* (1983) – Bombolo
* *[Crime in Formula One](/wiki/Crime_in_Formula_One "Crime in Formula One")* (1984) – Venticello
* *[Cop in Drag](/wiki/Cop_in_Drag "Cop in Drag")* (1984) – Franco 'Venticello' Bertarelli
* *Vacanze d'estate* (1985) – Cardinale
* *Giuro che ti amo* (1986) – (final film role)
